title: Tailwind v4 broken because PostCSS plugin is set to 'tailwindcss' instead of '@tailwindcss/postcss'
summary: Using the v3 PostCSS plugin name in a v4 project causes styles to silently fail or produce build errors.
mistake: Setting the PostCSS plugin to `tailwindcss` in `postcss.config.mjs` when the project uses Tailwind CSS v4. This is the v3 plugin name. In v4, the PostCSS plugin was renamed to `@tailwindcss/postcss`. The build may not error — it may silently fall back to v3 behavior or produce no utility classes at all. The agent sees no build error and reports success, but the styles are broken.
instead: Always check the installed Tailwind version (`node_modules/tailwindcss/package.json`). For v4+, the PostCSS config must use `@tailwindcss/postcss` as the plugin, not `tailwindcss`. Install with `npm install -D @tailwindcss/postcss`. Also replace `@tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;` with `@import "tailwindcss"` in the CSS entry point, and remove `autoprefixer` from PostCSS plugins (v4 includes it).
